text.title: Ads Analytics Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Ads Analytics Setup MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Event specification (name, params, trigger, owner) MUST be written before any tracking code. Code-first implementations are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Code-first events accumulate inconsistency; spec-first events stay coherent over years.

rule.statement: Consent Mode v2 MUST be implemented from day one (not retrofitted). Without it, GDPR audit exposure is unbounded.

rationale.source: Google Consent Mode v2 docs + GDPR practice

rule.rationale: Retrofit Consent Mode breaks historical data + invites compliance fines; from-day-one keeps both clean.

rule.statement: GA4 → BigQuery export MUST be linked on day one of property creation. Late-link loses raw events permanently.
rule.rationale: GA4 stores raw events only after BQ link; pre-link events are aggregated-only forever.

rule.statement: UTM naming MUST follow a documented convention (source/medium/campaign/term/content); free-form UTMs are rejected at tagging.
rule.rationale: Free-form UTMs (case / typo / synonym variants) destroy attribution within 2 quarters.

rule.statement: All events MUST be verified in DebugView before go-live. Un-verified events go to production silently broken.
rule.rationale: Silent broken events corrupt 30-60 days of data before discovery.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
